network.overall_strategy: This network is centered on the only experimentally demonstrated prebiotic route to histidine: erythrose + formamidine giving imidazole-4-acetaldehyde via Schiff-base formation and Amadori rearrangement, followed by Strecker conversion to histidine (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990). Because the literature shows no other confirmed complete abiotic route to histidine, the 10 pathways are constructed as distinct convergent network routes feeding the same bottleneck intermediate set rather than as 10 independent validated histidine syntheses. The network therefore emphasizes: (i) hydrothermal generation of simple C1/C2 feedstocks, (ii) surface concentration, formose, HCN, and imidazole-forming chemistries that converge on erythrose, formamidine, and imidazole-related hubs, and (iii) biochemical/prebiotic completion by the Shen Strecker route and downstream peptide assembly. Reactions unsupported as direct histidine-forming steps are explicitly marked as speculative connectors where needed, consistent with the literature finding that histidine is exceptionally elusive prebiotically.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal CO2 hydrogenation to formaldehyde/glycolaldehyde feedstocks

conditions.other: alkaline vent to subseafloor hydrothermal setting; H2-rich, anoxic

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-surface CO2 reduction and C-C coupling under Fischer-Tropsch-type and iron-sulfur mediated conditions generate reduced carbonyl feedstocks including formaldehyde-equivalent and glycolaldehyde-equivalent products.
reactions.reasoning: Hydrothermal generation of reduced C1/C2 organics is supported broadly for magnetite/greigite systems and provides plausible feedstock delivery to surface environments, although the exact formaldehyde/glycolaldehyde yields in vent settings are system-dependent; used here as a connector consistent with hydrothermal carbon fixation literature and the review's statement that formaldehyde and glycolaldehyde are highly plausible prebiotic intermediates. Direct link to histidine is speculative because no hydrothermal histidine synthesis is known (Huber & Wachtershauser, 1997; Kitadai, 2015; Green et al., 2023 review context).

reactions.name: Surface borate-stabilized aldol/formose access to erythrose from formaldehyde and glycolaldehyde

conditions.catalyst: borate minerals and clay concentration surfaces
conditions.other: wet-dry cycles; mildly alkaline pH 8-9; borate stabilization of pentose/tetrose pool

reactions.mechanism: Formose/aldol chemistry extends C1/C2 carbonyl feedstocks to tetrose sugars, with borate complexation stabilizing otherwise labile erythrose.
reactions.reasoning: Erythrose is required by the only demonstrated histidine synthesis. Its prebiotic access from formose-type chemistry and stabilization by borate are supported, though erythrose remains minor and fragile. This step follows the literature emphasis that erythrose is a plausible but problematic intermediate (Decker et al., 1982; Ricardo et al., 2004; Yi et al., 2023; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: HCN plus ammonia to formamidine under concentrating surface conditions

conditions.other: evaporitic concentration; transient accumulation; anoxic; formamidine highly labile

reactions.mechanism: HCN-derived amidine formation occurs under concentrated aqueous conditions, producing methanimidamide as a transient nitrogen-rich reagent.
reactions.reasoning: Formamidine is specifically invoked in the Shen histidine synthesis but criticized as prebiotically implausible because it hydrolyzes rapidly and the required 0.3 M concentration is unrealistic. This node is therefore necessary to represent the only demonstrated route but should be regarded as a severe bottleneck (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Shen-Amadori imidazole ring formation from erythrose and formamidine

conditions.catalyst: mineral concentration/stabilization surfaces; no dedicated catalytic mineral demonstrated
conditions.other: formamidine approximately 0.3 M in the laboratory synthesis; Schiff base formation followed by Amadori rearrangement; yield about 1.6% from erythrose

reactions.mechanism: Erythrose condenses with formamidine to a Schiff base, then undergoes internal redox isomerization (Amadori-type rearrangement) that constructs the imidazole ring and furnishes imidazole-4-acetaldehyde.
reactions.reasoning: This is the key literature-established bottleneck and the only demonstrated route to the direct histidine precursor imidazole-4-acetaldehyde. The use of mineral agents here reflects concentration/stabilization surfaces required by the schema; however, no mineral catalyst has been demonstrated specifically for this transformation. The chemistry and low yield are directly from the Shen studies; the plausibility concerns follow the later critique (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Strecker formation of histidine aminonitrile from imidazole-4-acetaldehyde

conditions.other: mildly basic aqueous setting; aldehyde-imine formation followed by cyanide addition

reactions.mechanism: The aldehyde condenses with ammonia to an imine, cyanide adds to form the alpha-aminonitrile precursor of histidine.
reactions.reasoning: This is the second stage of the Shen-Oró route and follows classical Strecker chemistry. While Strecker synthesis is broadly prebiotically plausible, for histidine it depends entirely on prior access to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, which remains the major unresolved issue (Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of histidine aminonitrile to histidine

conditions.other: aminonitrile hydrolysis completing Strecker sequence; overall reported histidine yield about 3.5% from erythrose

reactions.mechanism: Sequential nitrile hydration and hydrolysis convert the aminonitrile to the alpha-amino acid histidine.
reactions.reasoning: Completion of the only demonstrated prebiotic histidine synthesis. Product confirmation in the original work used HPLC and MS methods. The route remains chemically valid but prebiotically weak because precursor supply is problematic (Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Debus-Radziszewski imidazole formation from glyoxal, formaldehyde, and ammonia

conditions.other: mild aqueous conditions; acidic microenvironments may assist diimine formation

reactions.mechanism: Carbonyl-ammonia condensation proceeds through diimine-type intermediates to the imidazole heterocycle.
reactions.reasoning: This reaction is experimentally demonstrated and shows that imidazole rings are prebiotically accessible from abundant feedstocks. However, it does not generate the specifically functionalized imidazole-4-acetaldehyde required for histidine and therefore serves as a convergence-supporting imidazole branch rather than a direct histidine path (Oró et al., 1984; Kua et al., 2011).

reactions.name: HCN tetramerization to DAMN

conditions.other: evaporative concentration of HCN; anoxic surface pools

reactions.mechanism: HCN oligomerizes under concentrated aqueous conditions to the tetramer diaminomaleonitrile.
reactions.reasoning: DAMN is a robust prebiotic hub in HCN chemistry and supports imidazole/purine-related convergence. It is not a direct histidine precursor but documents the facile formation of nitrogen-rich imidazole precursors on the early Earth (Ferris & Orgel, 1966; Ferris & Kuder, 1970).

reactions.name: UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN

conditions.other: near-350 nm UV irradiation in ozone-free surface environment

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical isomerization of DAMN reorganizes the nitrile-rich framework into the aminoimidazole nitrile AICN.
reactions.reasoning: This classic HCN photochemistry provides a high-confidence abiotic route to aminoimidazole scaffolds and a conceptual link between purine chemistry and histidine-related imidazole chemistry, though not a direct route to histidine (Ferris & Orgel, 1966; Ferris & Kuder, 1970).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of AICN to AICA

conditions.other: aqueous hydrolysis of nitrile to carboxamide

reactions.mechanism: Nitrile hydrolysis yields the aminoimidazole carboxamide AICA, a purine-related intermediate.
reactions.reasoning: AICA/AICAR mark the evolutionary connection between purine and histidine metabolism, but the literature explicitly cautions that this does not solve prebiotic histidine synthesis. Included as a documented imidazole convergence branch (Ferris & Kuder, 1970; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Cyanide-driven FoDHA-CN cascade to an imidazole derivative

conditions.catalyst: none specifically required in the report; represented here with surface photochemical/concentration minerals per schema
conditions.other: pH 9.2 cyanide chemistry; reported 63% yield to an imidazole derivative from simple precursors

reactions.mechanism: A cyanide-triggered cascade forms multiple C-N bonds and closes an imidazole ring from nitrile-containing precursor chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: Green, Xu, and Sutherland (2023) showed an efficient prebiotic imidazole-forming cascade, arguably the most promising modern advance. However, no extension to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde or histidine has been demonstrated. The product is represented here at the level of an imidazole branch connector, not as a validated direct histidine precursor (Green et al., 2023).

reactions.name: Wet-dry peptide condensation of histidine to histidylhistidine

conditions.temperature: geothermal cycling; decomposition suppressed on mineral surfaces
conditions.other: wet-dry cycling on mineral interfaces or concentrated pools; peptide formation after histidine production

reactions.mechanism: Dehydrative condensation links two histidine molecules into a dipeptide under fluctuating concentration and thermal conditions.
reactions.reasoning: Histidine has been shown to self-assemble and undergo peptide-bond formation on mineral surfaces, and histidyl peptides have catalytic relevance in prebiotic chemistry. This downstream step is included to connect target formation to biochemical function, not as part of abiotic histidine synthesis itself (Madrigal-Trejo et al., 2021; White & Erickson, 1981; Astbury et al., 2024).

pathways.name: Canonical Shen route from hydrothermal C1/C2 feedstocks
pathways.description: Hydrothermal reduced carbon feedstocks are transferred to surface formose chemistry to make erythrose, which combines with formamidine to give imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, then Strecker chemistry yields histidine.

pathways.name: Surface-assembled Shen route emphasizing erythrose bottleneck
pathways.description: A surface-focused path in which formaldehyde/glycolaldehyde are concentrated into erythrose before entering the only confirmed histidine-forming sequence.

pathways.name: HCN-rich Shen route via direct formamidine convergence
pathways.description: HCN chemistry produces formamidine, converging with erythrose to access imidazole-4-acetaldehyde and then histidine.

pathways.name: Debus-imidazole informed branch feeding the histidine bottleneck conceptually
pathways.description: Imidazole ring chemistry from glyoxal/formaldehyde/ammonia demonstrates abiotic imidazole availability but still must converge on the Shen bottleneck to reach histidine.

pathways.name: DAMN-AICN branch converging on histidine only through Shen precursor chemistry
pathways.description: HCN oligomerization and photochemistry generate aminoimidazole scaffolds, illustrating convergent imidazole chemistry that still requires erythrose/formamidine chemistry for actual histidine formation.

pathways.name: FoDHA-CN imidazole branch plus Shen completion
pathways.description: The Sutherland high-yield imidazole-forming cascade is incorporated as a modern plausible imidazole source, but completion to histidine still passes through the sole demonstrated imidazole-4-acetaldehyde route.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-to-surface cross-environment route with HCN convergence
pathways.description: Hydrothermal carbon reduction supplies formaldehyde/glycolaldehyde, while surface HCN chemistry supplies formamidine; the two streams converge at imidazole-4-acetaldehyde.

pathways.name: Borate-stabilized erythrose accumulation route
pathways.description: This path emphasizes borate-assisted stabilization of the fragile erythrose intermediate before entry into the Shen sequence.

pathways.name: Purine-histidine evolutionary bridge route
pathways.description: AICN/AICA chemistry is used to represent the documented purine-side aminoimidazole network, which conceptually converges on histidine chemistry only when the Shen bottleneck is supplied independently.

pathways.name: Histidine to functional peptide continuation
pathways.description: After the sole validated histidine synthesis sequence, histidine enters biochemical assembly to form histidyl peptides with possible catalytic relevance.
